title: Cephaelis lecomtei
taxonomy: Cephaelis lecomtei belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee family. This family comprises over 600 genera and 13,000 species, many of which are native to tropical regions. Cephaelis lecomtei is a member of the Rubiaceae family and is classified within the genus Cephaelis. The genus Cephaelis is a group of plants that are primarily found in Central and South America, and some species have been introduced to other parts of the world. Cephaelis lecomtei is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the Cephaelis genus.

side_effects: Cephaelis lecomtei is also known as the Yohimbe tree, and its bark extract is used in traditional medicine. The bark extract of Cephaelis lecomtei contains yohimbine, a compound that can cause side effects such as anxiety, insomnia, and increased heart rate. High doses of yohimbine can lead to more severe side effects like hypertension, tremors, and seizures. Yohimbine may also interact with certain medications, including blood thinners, and worsen conditions like high blood pressure and heart disease. Therefore, individuals should consult a healthcare professional before using Cephaelis lecomtei or its extracts.
intro: Cephaelis lecomtei, also known as the Yunnan pu-erh tea plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote digestive health, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis lecomtei include polyphenols, catechins, and other alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea infusions and extracts, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to support overall health and well-being.
